name: Search Index AgentSite
description: A static AgentSite that feels queryable through a precomputed public-safe search index.
brief: Create a static GitHub Pages site that demonstrates a public-safe search index over recipes, artifacts, roadmap items, contracts, and plans. It should feel like a static intelligence pack without backend search, API keys, analytics, or private data.
heroHeadline: A static search layer for the AgentSite brain
heroLede: The page precomputes repo/site intelligence into JSON so visitors and future agents can search recipes, proof, roadmap items, plans, and contracts without a backend.
audience: Technical reviewers evaluating static intelligence patterns
visualDirection: dark product cockpit with static intelligence search, artifact gallery, and roadmap surfaces

sections.title: Public-safe index generated at build time
sections.body: The index is rebuilt from repo-local metadata during QA and checked before deployment.

sections.title: Future agents get faster orientation
sections.body: Recipes, artifacts, roadmap items, contracts, and plans become searchable without a server.

sections.title: Static safety stays explicit
sections.body: No secrets, private data, analytics, API keys, semantic claims, or live crawling are included.

proofArtifacts.label: Search index data
proofArtifacts.body: src/data/search-index.json is generated by npm run build:search-index and validated by npm run check:search-index.

proofArtifacts.label: Static search UI
proofArtifacts.body: src/components/SearchIndex.astro filters precomputed entries in the browser with no backend.

proofArtifacts.label: Visual QA
proofArtifacts.body: npm run test:visual exercises search and type filtering when the index surface is present.

allowedClaims: Repo-local QA scripts validate index data
approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Indexing private docs, credentials, secrets, or unpublished user data
Adding external search providers, API keys, server-side runtime, authentication, or a database
